justification: Official JEC press release explicitly names both Hassan and Hawley pressing AI companies to combat scammers, matching the evidence text.
Hassan's official press release names both Hassan and Banks introducing bipartisan AI security legislation to advance national security research.
Wikipedia article on Maggie Hassan documents that both she and Jeanne Shaheen are NH senators and former governors, but does not document a specific collaborative act between the two.
Senate Commerce Committee press release explicitly names Cantwell and Moran reintroducing the Small Business Artificial Intelligence Training Act of 2026.
Moran's official press release documents him joining Ricketts and other Republican colleagues in the Iran nuclear program letter, naming both senators.
Hickenlooper's official press release names both Hickenlooper and Capito reintroducing the VET AI Act directing NIST to develop AI evaluation guidelines.
Hickenlooper's press release names him and John Thune among the bipartisan group introducing the Artificial Intelligence Research, Innovation, and Accountability Act.
Klobuchar's press release names Hickenlooper and Klobuchar among the colleagues introducing the Artificial Intelligence Research, Innovation, and Accountability Act.
VET AI Act press release explicitly states the bill directs NIST to develop specifications and guidelines for third-party evaluators, documenting Hickenlooper's legislative collaboration with NIST.
Blumenthal's official press release names both Blumenthal and Hawley as sponsors of the GUARD Act to ban AI chatbots for children.
Murphy's press release explicitly names both Blumenthal and Murphy as co-sponsors of the GUARD Act bipartisan bill.
Britt's official press release names Blumenthal and Britt together as co-sponsors of the bipartisan GUARD Act protecting minors from AI chatbots.
Warner's official press release names both Blumenthal and Warner as co-sponsors of the GUARD Act bipartisan legislation.
Blumenthal's official press release names both Blumenthal and Blackburn as lead sponsors of the Kids Online Safety Act, first introduced in February 2022.
Rosen's official press release names both Rosen and Budd introducing the Five AIs Act to develop an AI initiative with U.S. intelligence allies.
